description: The provided image appears to be a generic update icon rather than the scientific figure. Based on the detailed paper context provided, the intended figure likely comprises transmission electron microscopy (TEM) characterization of near-rapidly solidified Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr (x=0, 3, 6 wt.%) low-density steel strips. The figure probably displays bright-field and dark-field micrographs alongside selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns showing: (1) nano-scale κ-carbide precipitates (perovskite structure, ~6–8 nm in 0Cr, decreasing volume fraction with Cr addition) within the γ-austenite matrix; (2) ordered DO3 phase particles (26–30 nm) in δ-ferrite; and (3) deformation microstructures with {111} planar slip bands exhibiting decreased spacing from 371±47 nm (0Cr) to 45±13 nm (6Cr) after 20% cold rolling. The figure demonstrates that Cr addition increases δ-ferrite content (3% to 18%) and promotes dense slip bands while suppressing κ-carbide formation, correlating with the observed mechanical property evolution.
